commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Yea, they turned back and tested God, and limited the Holy One of Israel. Hebrew, "set bounds to "or "marked "holding up to scorn, Hebrews vi. Genebrard thinks we might translate, "crucified "hithvu, as this is the root of Thau, which formerly resembled a cross. (Calmet) This would surely be one of the most striking enigmas. Jerome agrees with us. (Berthier) "They pushed on "concitaverunt, as God's wrath must be greatly excited by setting limits to his power and goodness.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
